name: Aniline
description: Aniline, phenylamine or aminobenzene is an organic compound with the formula C6H5NH2. Consisting of a phenyl group attached to an amino group, aniline is the prototypical aromatic amine. Being a precursor to many industrial chemicals, its main use is in the manufacture of precursors to polyurethane. Like most volatile amines, it possesses the somewhat unpleasant odour of rotten fish. It ignites readily, burning with a smoky flame characteristic of aromatic compounds. Aniline is colorless, but it slowly oxidizes and resinifies in air, giving a red-brown tint to aged samples.

interactions.name: Cyproterone acetate
interactions.description: The serum concentration of Aniline can be decreased when it is combined with Cyproterone acetate.

interactions.name: Nicotine
interactions.description: The metabolism of Aniline can be decreased when combined with Nicotine.

interactions.name: Isoniazid
interactions.description: The metabolism of Aniline can be decreased when combined with Isoniazid.

interactions.name: Clotrimazole
interactions.description: The metabolism of Aniline can be decreased when combined with Clotrimazole.

interactions.name: Ticlopidine
interactions.description: The metabolism of Aniline can be decreased when combined with Ticlopidine.
